articles.title: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
articles.snippet: As the war in the Middle East enters its fourth week, President Trump says the U.S. is considering 'winding down' military efforts, as it also seeks to ease the energy crisis by lifting sanctions on Iranian oil stranded at sea.

articles.title: War in Iran Reveals Strait of Hormuz's Weakness as an Oil Bottleneck
articles.snippet: At just 35 miles wide, the Strait proved vulnerable. It's the latest evidence of how dependent the global economy is on a handful of choke points.

articles.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
articles.snippet: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of 'reckless strikes'.

articles.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors, jury finds
articles.snippet: A San Francisco jury concludes Musk's claims in 2022 were damaging to a group of Twitter investors.

articles.title: Over 5,500 told to evacuate flooding in Hawaii as officials warn dam could fail
articles.snippet: Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated communities and prompted evacuation orders for more than 5,500 people in towns north of Honolulu. Officials are warning about the possible failure of a 120-year-old dam.

articles.title: A massive border wall expansion is underway
articles.snippet: The aggressive pace of expansion has alarmed advocates who say the construction will destroy pristine country, threaten endangered species, and cut off access to sacred Indigenous and archaeological sites.

articles.title: Judge Rules Pentagon Restrictions on Press Are Unconstitutional
articles.snippet: A federal judge tossed parts of the Pentagon's restrictions on news outlets, saying they violated the First Amendment, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.

articles.title: How the Iran war threatens global food supply
articles.snippet: About a third of all fertilizer shipped globally passes through the Strait of Hormuz. Now shipping is all-but stopped and this could have repercussions for the global food supply.

articles.title: Five questions that still need answering about the meningitis outbreak
articles.snippet: The size and speed of the outbreak which has now affected 29 people, killing two of them, has been labelled 'unprecedented'.

articles.title: Woman charged with murder after allegedly taking abortion pills, going to hospital
articles.snippet: The 31-year-old Georgia woman went to the emergency room with severe pain after allegedly taking abortion pills at home, according to police and court records.
